captured_from: Public Anthropic API docs (messages object) — synthetic anonymised examples mirroring documented shape; WAL sampling deferred (see Phase 2 checkpoint notes).

examples.notes: Plain non-streaming Anthropic messages response. Anthropic uses input_tokens/output_tokens (not prompt_tokens/completion_tokens) — those map to our canonical prompt_tokens/completion_tokens. cache_read_input_tokens → cached_tokens (read-from-cache); cache_creation_input_tokens → cache_creation_tokens (write-to-cache). Anthropic does NOT return total_tokens; downstream code computes it. stop_sequence is the matched string when stop_reason='stop_sequence', not the reason itself — UNKNOWN.

Streaming chunk shape — `content_block_delta` event payload. The text-delta is incremental `content` tokens. message-level metadata (id/model/usage) arrives in separate `message_start` / `message_delta` / `message_stop` events; this fixture captures the most common per-token chunk.

Extended-thinking response. content[0] is the thinking block (label=thinking_content); content[1] is the final assistant text (label=content). The CRF EXCLUSIVE_GROUPS['primary_content']=('content','thinking_content') prior allows BOTH to coexist across content[*] siblings even though they're from the same exclusive group — exclusion is per-sibling-slot, and these are at different array indices. `signature` is the per-thinking-block opaque token Anthropic uses for verification, not a model fingerprint — UNKNOWN.
